title: Vault CLI Cheat Sheet
description: Essential commands for managing secrets with HashiCorp Vault.

commands.description: Authenticate to Vault using a token or other method.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to authenticate to Vault to perform operations.

commands.description: Check the status of the Vault server.
commands.scenario: Use to verify if the Vault server is initialized and unsealed.

commands.description: Enable a key-value secrets engine at a given path.
commands.scenario: Use to start storing secrets in a new key-value store.

commands.description: Store a secret in the key-value store.
commands.scenario: Use to save a new secret or update an existing one.

commands.description: Retrieve a secret from the key-value store.
commands.scenario: Use to access a stored secret's value.

commands.description: Delete a secret from the key-value store.
commands.scenario: Use to remove a secret that is no longer needed.

commands.description: Unseal the Vault server with a key share.
commands.scenario: Use to unseal Vault after a restart or initialization.
Use to set up a new Vault server for the first time.

commands.description: Create or update a policy from a file.
commands.scenario: Use to define access control policies for Vault.
Use to see which auth methods are available in Vault.

commands.description: Revoke all leases under a specific prefix.
commands.scenario: Use to revoke access to secrets under a specific path.

commands.description: Enable file audit logging to a specified path.
commands.scenario: Use to track access and operations in Vault for security auditing.

commands.description: Create a new token with a specific policy attached.
commands.scenario: Use to generate a token for accessing Vault with defined permissions.
